briefdescription.para: The instance of the Interrupt Controller.
This function is used to initialize the TRNG driver.

simplesect.para: Error code on any error XST_SUCCESS on success

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the TRNG Instance.

simplesect.para: NULL or Pointer to the TRNG Instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the free slot ID from the request QueueID.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the free slot index.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the Queue ID.
This function is used to prepare the descriptor data for the requested crypto operation.
This function is used to update the Queue info for the EnQueued requests.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the queue info structure
Request ID for the EnQueued request API

listitem.para: XPKI_UNSUPPORTED_OPS - If the requested operation is not supported

briefdescription.para: This function is used the get the ECC curve length.

parameterdescription.para: Type of ECC curve (NIST-P192, 256, P384, and P521)

briefdescription.para: This function copy the user-pointed data into the Request queue buffer in the below order(as per the PKI Spec) to generate the signature.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the Request queue input address
Pointer to the Generate Signature Descriptor Data offset

briefdescription.para: This function is used to validate the elliptic curve signature generation input parameters.
This function is used to initialize and trigger the PKI Operations.

parameterdescription.para: RQ page offset value which points to Crypto OP descriptor buffer

briefdescription.para: This function load the user-pointed crypto data (inputs) into the Request queue buffer (as per the PKI Spec).

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the queue info structure.

listitem.para: XPKI_UNSUPPORTED_OPS - If the requested operation is not supported.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to validate the elliptic curve signature verification input parameters.
This function copy the user-pointed data into the Request queue buffer in the below order(as per the PKI Spec) to validate the signature.

para.verbatim: -Public key(Qx, Qy) -Signature(SignR, SignS) -Data Hash -Verify Signature Descriptor Data

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the Request queue input address
Pointer to the Verify Signature Descriptor Data offset

briefdescription.para: This function is used the get the ECC curve Order.

parameterdescription.para: Type of ECC curve (NIST-P192, P256, P384, and P521)

briefdescription.para: This function is used to fill the RQ to generate the Private key for the request curve.

parameterdescription.para: Type of ECC curve (NIST-P192, P256, P384, and P521)
Pointer to the Request queue input address
Pointer to the Point-Multiplication Descriptor Data offset

briefdescription.para: This function copy the user-pointed data into the Request queue buffer in the below order(as per the PKI Spec) to perform modulus addition.

para.verbatim: - Curve Order - TRGN Generated Random value(A) - Value B = 1 -fixed - Modular addition Descriptor Data

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the Request queue input address
Pointer to the modular addition Descriptor Data offset

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the ECC curve generator points.

parameterdescription.para: Type of ECC curve (NIST-P192, P256, P384, and P521)

briefdescription.para: This function is used the get the ECC curve type for the given operation.

parameterdescription.para: Type of ECC curve (NIST-P192, P256, P384, and P521)

memberdef.argsstring: (XPki_EcdsaCrvType CrvType, u8 *PrivKey, UINTPTR Addr, u32 *DescPtr, u64 *EcdsaReqVa)
memberdef.name: XPki_EcdsaGenPubKey

briefdescription.para: This function is used to fill the RQ to generate the Public key for the request curve.

parameterdescription.para: Type of ECC curve (NIST-P192, P256, P384, and P521)
Pointer to the Request queue input address
Pointer to the Point-Multiplication Descriptor Data offset

listitem.para:
XST_SUCCESS - On success
XST_FAILURE - On failure

briefdescription.para: This function is used to free the Queue info for the DeQueued requests.

parameterdescription.para: Request ID for the DeQueued request API

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the crypto operation results from the PKI Queue.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the queue info structure
Unique request ID Filled by the XilPki_EnQueue() API

listitem.para: XPKI_UNSUPPORTED_OPS - If the requested operation is not supported

briefdescription.para: This function copy the ecdsa generated signature data into the user-pointed buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Type of ECC curve(NIST-P192, NIST-P256, P384 and P521)
Pointer to the Request queue out address.

briefdescription.para: This function copy the ecdsa generated signature data into the user-pointed buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Type of ECC curve(NIST-P192, NIST-P256, P384 and P521)
Pointer to the Request queue out address

briefdescription.para: This function copy the ecdsa generated signature data into the user-pointed buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Type of ECC curve(NIST-P192, NIST-P256, P384 and P521)
Pointer to the Request queue out address

briefdescription.para: This function implements the interrupt handler.
This function setups the interrupt system such that interrupts can occur for the PKI.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the instance of the PKI which is going to be connected to the interrupt controller.

briefdescription.para: This function initialize the requested queue.

simplesect.para: -XST_SUCCESS - On success -XPKI_INVALID_PARAM - On invalid argument -XPKI_ERROR_UNALIGN_ADDR - On Request/Completion queue Address are not Word aligned. -XPKI_INVALID_QUEUE_ID - If the provided QueueID is Invalid. -XST_FAILURE - On failure

briefdescription.para: This function is use to notify the user if the submitted request completed.
This function is used to validate the given request ID against with submitted request IDs.
This function is used to assign the page address to the PKI Queues.
This function reset the PKI module.
This function performs the PKI module soft reset.
This function performs the PKI module initialization.

listitem.para: XPKI_ERROR_UNALIGN_ADDR - On Request/Completion queue Address are not Word aligned.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to disable access to the PKI Hardware.
This function is used to generate the Random number for the give size.

parameterdescription.para: Required Random Number length in bytes.
Pointer to store the Random Number.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to submit the crypto operation to the PKI Queue.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the queue info structure
Pointer to the RequestID. if the request is successfully submitted it filled with unique id

listitem.para: XPKI_UNSUPPORTED_OPS - If the requested operation is not supported.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the crypto operation results from the PKI Queue.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the queue info structure
Unique request ID Filled by the XilPki_EnQueue() API.

listitem.para: XPKI_UNSUPPORTED_OPS - If the requested operation is not supported.

briefdescription.para: This function copy the user-pointed data into the Request queue buffer in the below order(as per the PKI Spec) to generate the Public-key.

para.verbatim: -Private key -Generator points(Gx, Gy) -Point Multiplication Descriptor Data

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the request queue input address
Pointer to the Point-Multiplication Descriptor Data offset

para.preformatted: This file contains the implementation of the interface functions for the PKI ECDSA hardware module. MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes ----- ---- -------- ------------------------------------------------------ 1.0 Nava 12/05/22 Initial Release 1.1 Nava 06/06/23 Fix the issues relevant to the pki mux selection/deselection logic. 2.0 Nava 06/21/23 Added PKI multi-queue support for ECC operations. 2.0 Nava 08/02/23 Added a new API XPki_GetVersion() to access the library version info. 2.0 Nava 09/06/23 Updated the XPki_GetVersion() API prototype to inline with other secure library version info API's. 2.0 Nava 09/07/23 Fixed issues with IRQ signal. 2.0 Nava 09/11/23 Fixed doxygen warnings. 2.1 Nava 12/07/23 Corrected the PKI_ECC_NIST_P384_KEY_PRIV_GEN_CMD value. 2.1 Nava 12/27/23 Updated the XPki_DeQueueData() API to return the proper error status for CopyEcdsa* operations. 2.1 Nava 01/03/24 Fixed security issues relevant to instance pointer NULL check. 2.2 kpt 01/09/24 Updated option for non-blocking trng reseed 2.2 Nava 06/12/24 Added support for system device-tree flow. 2.4 Arvd 02/04/26 Fixed codespell errors.
